commands.note: 2026-06-03: re-enabled after being toggled off. Confirmed live edge-decay gate is reading from v25-bot-excl-climbing/shadow (not v48-climb-signal) — intentional retention of that signal pointer for v48a/v48b. Reason: v48-climb-signal only has n=6 closed trades, well below the 25-trade LOW-N threshold, so its own flag would block v48a/v48b indefinitely. v25-base has n=531 closed trades (capped at 200 in edge-decay) and produces a stable, usable flag (currently STABLE: recent_30 -3.74 vs all -4.73, +0.99pp delta). v48-climb-signal still serves as the always-on ungated control — once it accumulates >=25 trades, we can revisit whether to switch the gate pointer to its own flag (matching the original V48 design).

2026-06-03: gate firing correctly. v25-bot-excl-climbing/shadow is STABLE (recent_30 -3.74 vs all -4.73, +0.99pp delta — well within the ±5pp STABLE band), so strength_only blocks every entry (n=0 trades, 117 cumulative gate skips). This is the designed behavior under STABLE: strict gate, wait for actual STRENGTHENING. Signal pointer retained at v25-bot-excl-climbing/shadow (not v48-climb-signal) — see v48-climb-signal journal for rationale (v48 baseline has too few trades to produce a non-LOW-N flag).

2026-06-03: gate firing correctly. With v25-base flag STABLE, v48b allows entries through the edge-decay gate (skip_decaying only blocks DECAYING). Cumulative: 92 gate skips during periods when v25-base flag dipped to DECAYING earlier in the window; 10 trades during STABLE windows. Per the count math vs v48a (117 skips, 25 more than v48b), ~25 STABLE-period grads passed the gate but most then failed downstream (filter pipeline / capacity / momentum row), leaving 10 actual trades. Signal pointer retained at v25-bot-excl-climbing/shadow per the v48-climb-signal journal rationale.
